items.title: Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Powell if he doesn’t step down by May 15
items.standfirst: In a Fox Business interview, Trump says he will fire Powell if he does not vacate his board seat when his term ends.
items.content: In a Fox Business interview, Trump says he will fire Powell if he does not vacate his board seat when his term ends.

items.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.
items.content: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank. (Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)

items.title: Trump threatens to fire Fed chair Jerome Powell amid pressure campaign
items.standfirst: US president says he has ‘held back’ on firing the head of the Federal Reserve leading up to end of Powell’s term in MaySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDonald Trump threatened to fire Jerome Powell if he stays on as US Federal Reserve chair past the end of his tenure and doubled down on a criminal investigation into renovations of the central bank’s headquarters.As the White House pushes Trump’s new nominee to take charge of the Fed, Kevin Warsh, Powell has a month left in the role. The possibility of Powell staying on as chair past 15 May, the official end of his term, has grown amid mounting scrutiny of Trump’s approach to the Fed in the Senate, which is required to approve Warsh’s nomination. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says he has ‘held back’ on firing the head of the Federal Reserve leading up to end of Powell’s term in MaySign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxDonald Trump threatened to fire Jerome Powell if he stays on as US Federal Reserve chair past the end of his tenure and doubled down on a criminal investigation into renovations of the central bank’s headquarters.As the White House pushes Trump’s new nominee to take charge of the Fed, Kevin Warsh, Powell has a month left in the role. The possibility of Powell staying on as chair past 15 May, the official end of his term, has grown amid mounting scrutiny of Trump’s approach to the Fed in the Senate, which is required to approve Warsh’s nomination. clusters.title: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
clusters.neutral_headline: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
bullet_summary: A student who was 14 and armed with five guns opened fire in a Turkish school a day after another shooter injured 16 people and killed himself in another school, officials said
A student has killed nine people and wounded 13 others in Turkey's second school shooting in two days, officials said

items.title: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
items.standfirst: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.
items.content: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

items.title: Eighth grader kills 9, wounds 13 in Turkey school shooting
items.standfirst: A student who was 14 and armed with five guns opened fire in a Turkish school a day after another shooter injured 16 people and killed himself in another school, officials said.
items.content: A student who was 14 and armed with five guns opened fire in a Turkish school a day after another shooter injured 16 people and killed himself in another school, officials said.

items.title: Student kills at least nine people in Turkey's second school shooting in two days
items.standfirst: A 14-year-old student fatally shot at least nine people, including eight of his fellow students, and wounded at least 13 others at a middle school in southeastern Turkey. This is the country’s second school shooting in two days. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports on Turkey’s deadliest school shooting.
items.content: A 14-year-old student fatally shot at least nine people, including eight of his fellow students, and wounded at least 13 others at a middle school in southeastern Turkey. This is the country’s second school shooting in two days. NBC News' Raf Sanchez reports on Turkey’s deadliest school shooting.

clusters.title: Kanye West Marseille concert postponed after anti-Semitic remarks
clusters.neutral_headline: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
bullet_summary: 🔎 US rapper #Kanye West said on Tuesday he was ​postponing his show in Marseille, France's second-biggest city, until further notice
Ye has postponed a concert in France that was scheduled for this summer, after officials in the country said he was not welcome and threatened to ban him over his antisemitic comments and songs
It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country

items.title: Kanye West Marseille concert postponed after anti-Semitic remarks

items.content: 🔎 US rapper #Kanye West said on Tuesday he was ​postponing his show in Marseille, France's second-biggest city, until further notice.

items.title: Kanye West postpones France concert after minister's block call
items.standfirst: US rapper Kanye West postponed late Tuesday his concert in the French city of Marseille, following reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance due to his anti-Semitic remarks.
items.content: US rapper Kanye West postponed late Tuesday his concert in the French city of Marseille, following reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance due to his anti-Semitic remarks.

clusters.title: Mediators move to extend US-Iran ceasefire, Trump says talks could resume soon
clusters.neutral_headline: US-Iran peace talks could resume in next two days, Trump says
bullet_summary: President Trump said a second round of direct U
After an interview discussing prospects for negotiations, the reporter said the president had called her back “with an update”

items.title: Mediators move to extend US-Iran ceasefire, Trump says talks could resume soon
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said talks with Iran to end the war could soon resume and end in a deal, telling the world to watch out for an "amazing two days", while US forces imposing a blockade turned back vessels leaving Iranian ports. With the prospect of US and Iranian officials returning to Pakistan for more talks, Vice President JD Vance, who led negotiations that ended on Sunday with no breakthrough, said he felt positive about where things ​stood.
items.content: President Donald Trump said talks with Iran to end the war could soon resume and end in a deal, telling the world to watch out for an "amazing two days", while US forces imposing a blockade turned back vessels leaving Iranian ports. With the prospect of US and Iranian officials returning to Pakistan for more talks, Vice President JD Vance, who led negotiations that ended on Sunday with no breakthrough, said he felt positive about where things ​stood.

items.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
items.standfirst: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.
items.content: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)

items.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
items.standfirst: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
items.content: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)

items.title: US-Iran peace talks could resume in next two days, Trump says
items.standfirst: US president says negotiations could restart in Islamabad under ‘fantastic’ Pakistani army chief Asim Munir• Middle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has said that US-Iranian peace talks could resume in Islamabad over the next two days, and complimented the work of Pakistan’s army chief as mediator.The US president was speaking on Tuesday to a New York Post reporter who had gone to Islamabad for the first round of ceasefire talks over the weekend. After an interview discussing prospects for negotiations, the reporter said the president had called her back “with an update”. Continue reading...

items.content: The S&P 500 hit a new all-time high Wednesday, a remarkable turnaround for stocks while the war with Iran drives up energy prices and cuts into global growth.

clusters.title: Attempted arson attack a bid to scare British Jews, says leader of London synagogue
clusters.neutral_headline: Manhunt after attempted arson attack on synagogue
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Attempted arson attack a bid to scare British Jews, says leader of London synagogue
items.standfirst: Met seeking two suspects and says overnight incident in Finchley being treated as antisemitic hate crimeA suspected attempted firebomb attack on a north London synagogue was a bid to intimidate British Jews, a leader at the place of worship has said, vowing that they will continue to work to “build bridges”.The Metropolitan police said a manhunt was under way after two people “wearing dark clothing and balaclavas” approached Finchley Reform Synagogue (FRS) just after midnight on Wednesday and threw a brick and two bottles suspected to contain petrol at the building. Continue reading...
items.content: Met seeking two suspects and says overnight incident in Finchley being treated as antisemitic hate crimeA suspected attempted firebomb attack on a north London synagogue was a bid to intimidate British Jews, a leader at the place of worship has said, vowing that they will continue to work to “build bridges”.The Metropolitan police said a manhunt was under way after two people “wearing dark clothing and balaclavas” approached Finchley Reform Synagogue (FRS) just after midnight on Wednesday and threw a brick and two bottles suspected to contain petrol at the building. Continue reading...

items.title: Manhunt after attempted arson attack on synagogue
items.standfirst: Police say two suspects threw petrol-filled bottles at Finchley Reform Synagogue but they did not ignite.
items.content: Police say two suspects threw petrol-filled bottles at Finchley Reform Synagogue but they did not ignite.

clusters.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
items.standfirst: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries.Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue reading...
items.content: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries.Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.
items.content: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.

clusters.title: Paris art enthusiast wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
clusters.neutral_headline: Paris art enthusiast wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Paris art enthusiast wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
items.standfirst: Ari Hodara initially thought it might be a hoax after winning raffle he found out about by chance while dining out A Picasso painting worth more than €1m (£870,000) has been won in a raffle by a software engineer from Paris who thought the whole thing might be a hoax.Ari Hodara learned he was the winner of the raffle on Tuesday when he answered a video call from Christie’s auction house in Paris. “How do I check that it’s not a hoax?” the 58 year-old asked when he was told he was the new owner of the 1941 work by the Spanish master. Continue reading...

items.content: "How do I know this isn't a prank?" the winner asked when he answered a video call from Christie's auction house in Paris.

clusters.title: Pope heads to Cameroon with message of peace for conflict zone
clusters.neutral_headline: Pope heads to Cameroon with message of peace for conflict zone

items.title: Pope heads to Cameroon with message of peace for conflict zone
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV left Algeria on Wednesday headed for Cameroon for a four-day trip that includes a visit to the epicentre of a nearly decade-long separatist conflict, where he is due to bring a message of peace. FRANCE 24's Indira Ayuk reports from Yaoundé, Cameroon.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV left Algeria on Wednesday headed for Cameroon for a four-day trip that includes a visit to the epicentre of a nearly decade-long separatist conflict, where he is due to bring a message of peace. FRANCE 24's Indira Ayuk reports from Yaoundé, Cameroon.

items.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
items.standfirst: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
